construct: Creates crafts and visual art forms applicable to real-life situations using appropriate artistic skills.

subSkills.label: Illustrates history of Art and Design in human development; demonstrates contemporary craftsmanship and resources in entrepreneurship skills

elementsOfConstruct.description: Illustrates technological and communicative art skills

subSkills.label: Demonstrates abilities in using digital tools and software to create and edit visual content; applies technology to produce high-quality, visually appealing illustrations
subSkills.note: ECZ Exemplar 2 (labeled EoC2) content is actually about identifying cultural significance of traditional crafts, not digital/graphic composition -- likely a documentation inconsistency in the source PDF. Flagged, not silently resolved.

subSkills.label: Draws or paints images from natural or man-made objects as a total composition, using various techniques and mediums for communication, aesthetics and entrepreneurship purposes

components.structureNotes: Continuous Assessment Tasks across Form 2-3 (19 tasks total, all 4 EoC) plus a Course work Project (1 task, 50 marks) spanning both forms. 2-year duration.
Continuous Assessment Tasks across Form 2-3 (19 tasks total, all 4 EoC) plus a Course work Project (1 task, 50 marks) spanning both forms. 2-year duration.

Section A: Part I Short Answers/Phrases [20]; Part II Competence Practice (drawing/painting on a cross-cutting issue) [20]. Section B: Picture Study [20]. Section C: 2 essay tasks (1 compulsory + 1 chosen) x [10] = [20]. Marks by EoC: EoC1=32, EoC2=24, EoC3=27, EoC4=17 (out of 100, across all Bloom levels). Duration 2h45.

rules: SBA scored by teachers per the School Based Assessment Guidelines for Examination for Certificate of Secondary Education Ordinary Level - Practical Subjects.
Final Examination marked centrally by ECZ using ECZ-provided marking schemes.
Candidates must sit both components (SBA and Final Exam) to receive an overall grade -- absence from either forfeits the overall grade.
Group marks are not allowed for practical assessment tasks; each candidate assessed individually.
All artefacts treated as examination scripts -- not to be tampered with, retained at least six months after scoring before disposal.
Felt pens and markers must not be used in the examination room.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Local art museum facing vandalism/theft threatening cultural heritage: describe how a commissioned art piece raises awareness, analyse impact of art theft, discuss museum preservation, list craft pieces to address the issue

Zambian visual artists losing traditional functional-artefact craft skills to modern technology (wood/woven fabric artefacts shown in a figure): identify cultural significance, explain importance of preserving crafts, suggest promotion/sale ideas, discuss cultural impact of decline, recommend government preservation action
